topic_title: Acceptable error — defining the failure threshold tolerable for a use case

required_sections.notes: All four required sections present and well above minimum word counts.

sampled_claims.claim: acceptable AI error rate was 6.8% vs 11.3% for humans
threshold set tight against test-set will be breached in production
alert fatigue can increase overall error rates
confidence threshold is a tunable parameter
confidence scores not calibrated probabilities unless explicitly calibrated
stakeholders hold AI to stricter standards than human practitioners
asymmetric cost: false positive vs false negative examples
sampled_claims.note: Illustrative examples in §5.2 table — not new empirical claims; no citation required.
Conceptual definition, not an empirical claim.

verdict: PASS — all six checks clear on cycle 1. Required sections present and substantial. Citation density 80% on sampled claims (threshold 60%). No forward references; 9.11 named-and-deferred only. Word count 2920 within standard range. Scope stays on acceptable error threshold-setting. All new terms defined on first use; prior vocabulary (alert fatigue, Judgment Framework) correctly back-referenced.
